approach: AI-assisted sub-day task blocks — not sprint-based. Claude Code executes all implementation; human resolves console-only gates and approves phase transitions.

phases.name: Foundation
objectives: GitHub repo created at praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise (public)
CloudFormation stack deployed: S3 input + output buckets (S3 Block Public Access enabled), IAM role
Bedrock model access enabled: amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0, amazon.titan-image-generator-v2:0, anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6
Python venv with all pinned dependencies installed
Claude Code scaffolding complete: CLAUDE.md, 8 skill stubs (.claude/skills/), hooks (.claude/settings.json), MCP servers (.mcp.json)
deliverables: infra/cloudformation/stack.yaml — S3 input + output buckets with Block Public Access, IAM role, AWS Budget alarm
requirements.txt — all deps pinned (boto3, pillow, anthropic, pytest, ruff, jsonschema, pip-audit)
.claude/skills/ — 8 skill stub files (run-pipeline, pipeline-status, view-results, deploy, teardown, health-check, run-tests, generate-demo)
.claude/settings.json — PostToolUse lint hook, Stop test gate hook, PreToolUse Bash guard hook
.mcp.json — AWS IaC MCP server (uv stdio) and AWS Knowledge MCP server (remote HTTP)
CLAUDE.md — repo purpose, skill invocations, architecture summary
Initial repo structure committed and pushed to praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise
success_criteria: aws cloudformation describe-stacks --stack-name pai-exercise returns CREATE_COMPLETE
aws sts get-caller-identity --profile pai-exercise returns account 730007904340
aws bedrock list-foundation-models --profile pai-exercise lists nova-canvas-v1:0 and claude-sonnet-4-6
python -c 'import boto3, PIL, anthropic, jsonschema; print("deps OK")' exits 0
git remote -v shows origin pointing to github.com/praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise
DECISION GATE G-001 PASSED before proceeding
dependencies: HUMAN REQUIRED: Enable Bedrock model access in AWS console before any Bedrock API calls
HUMAN REQUIRED: Run aws configure --profile pai-exercise and verify sts get-caller-identity
HUMAN REQUIRED: gh auth status shows authenticated as praeducer; run gh auth login if not
Node.js LTS installed (for MCP npx/uv commands)
AWS CLI 2.x installed and on PATH
risks: G-001 BLOCKING: Bedrock model access console enablement may take up to 24h for first-time request — do this before writing any pipeline code
GitHub repo creation requires authenticated gh CLI — verify gh auth status before P-001 starts

phases.name: Core Pipeline
objectives: Working end-to-end pipeline: SKU brief JSON → 1 image per product (1:1 ratio) in S3
Pipeline invokable via /run-pipeline skill
MINIMUM DEMO STATE: one /run-pipeline invocation produces ≥2 images in S3
deliverables: src/schemas/sku_brief_schema.json — JSON schema with required product fields
inputs/sample_sku_brief.json — 2+ products (e.g., Organic Trail Mix Original and Dark Chocolate)
src/pipeline/sku_parser.py — parse + validate SKU brief against schema
src/pipeline/asset_manager.py — S3 read/write, output key builder {SKU}/{Region}/{format}/
src/pipeline/prompt_constructor.py — f-string templates with product name, attributes, region
src/pipeline/image_generator.py — dev tier (Titan V2), 1:1 ratio, retry/backoff (3 attempts, 2^n seconds)
src/pipeline/text_overlay.py — Pillow text rendering for 1:1 layout (product name, attributes, regulatory footer)
src/pipeline/output_manager.py — S3 upload + manifest.json (E-006) local and S3 write
src/pipeline/run_pipeline.py — orchestrator: SKU parser → prompt → generate → overlay → upload → manifest
src/pipeline/text_reasoning.py — AnthropicBedrock client (C-012) for prompt construction assistance
.claude/skills/run-pipeline.md — skill updated to invoke run_pipeline.py with sku-brief-path
success_criteria: /run-pipeline inputs/sample_sku_brief.json completes without error
S3 output bucket shows ≥2 images (one per product) under {sku_id}/{region}/front_label/
Pipeline run manifest written to both S3 and local outputs/runs/
Human inspects images and confirms text overlay is readable (MINIMUM DEMO STATE GATE G-002)
PAI_DRY_RUN=1 run completes with zero Bedrock API calls
dependencies: P-001 complete — G-001 passed, CloudFormation deployed, Bedrock accessible
risks: Pillow text overlay coordinate mapping for 1:1 ratio may require 1-2 AI iteration passes — timebox to 45 minutes
AnthropicBedrock client requires explicit aws_region='us-east-1' — does not read ~/.aws/config for region automatically

phases.name: Output Quality
objectives: All 3 aspect ratios (1:1 front label, 9:16 back label, 16:9 wraparound) working
Image caching (~/.cache/pai-pipeline/) reduces development iteration time
--dry-run mode end-to-end (zero Bedrock cost for testing)
2+ products × 3 ratios = 6+ images per pipeline run, organized in S3
deliverables: src/pipeline/image_generator.py — updated for all 3 model tiers and all 3 aspect ratios with dimension mapping
src/pipeline/text_overlay.py — updated for 9:16 and 16:9 layouts (distinct text zones per ratio)
src/pipeline/run_pipeline.py — iterates all ratios × all products
Image caching: SHA-256 hash key, ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/ directory
--dry-run flag: 1×1 placeholder image, no Bedrock calls, dry_run=true in manifest
.claude/skills/pipeline-status.md — reads outputs/runs/*.json manifests, shows run table
.claude/skills/view-results.md — lists S3 objects per SKU, optionally opens images
success_criteria: Full run produces ≥6 images (2 products × 3 ratios) in S3 organized as {SKU}/{region}/front_label/, back_label/, wraparound/
Human inspects all 3 aspect ratios — text overlay readable across all formats (G-003 gate)
Second run with same brief is faster (cache hit observed)
PAI_DRY_RUN=1 completes with no S3 image uploads and manifest shows dry_run: true
/pipeline-status shows recent run table in Claude Code
dependencies: P-002 complete — G-002 passed, pipeline producing ≥2 images
risks: Pillow text layout differences across 3 aspect ratios — 9:16 tall format and 16:9 wide format require distinct coordinate zones; timebox to 1 hour total across all 3 layouts
Cache key collision possible if prompt hashing implementation has bugs — verify with identical + slightly different prompts

phases.name: CI/CD and Testing
objectives: GitHub Actions ci.yml passes: ruff lint + pytest unit tests + pip-audit on every push
GitHub Actions deploy.yml runs CloudFormation update on main push
pytest test suite covers all 5 pipeline modules (unit) + 1 integration test (dry-run)
Claude Code hooks verified: auto-lint on file edit, test gate on Stop
deliverables: .github/workflows/ci.yml — lint (ruff) + pytest (unit) + pip-audit; triggers on push and PR
.github/workflows/deploy.yml — CloudFormation deploy; triggers on main push only
tests/test_sku_parser.py — valid/invalid brief, 2-product brief, field validation
tests/test_prompt_constructor.py — prompt contains product name, attributes, region
tests/test_image_generator.py — dry-run returns bytes, cache hit skips Bedrock
tests/test_text_overlay.py — output is bytes, correct dimensions per ratio
tests/test_asset_manager.py — S3 key builder produces correct structure
tests/test_integration.py — marked @pytest.mark.integration; full pipeline --dry-run
Makefile or pyproject.toml — make audit, make test, make lint targets
GitHub Actions SHA-pinned action versions in both workflow files
success_criteria: git push origin main triggers ci.yml — green checkmark within 2 minutes
pytest tests/ -v -m 'not integration' passes with all unit tests green
ruff check src/ exits 0 (zero lint errors)
pip-audit exits 0 (no high/critical vulnerabilities in pinned deps)
Claude Code PostToolUse hook auto-lints a changed .py file (observe in Claude Code output)
DECISION GATE G-004 PASSED: GitHub Actions tab shows green on main
dependencies: P-003 complete — G-003 passed, all 3 ratios working
risks: GitHub Actions AWS credentials setup (OIDC or Secrets) requires IAM Identity Provider creation in console — human task, 15 minutes
pip-audit may flag pinned dependency with known CVE — upgrade to patch version and re-pin

phases.name: Docs and Demo
objectives: README renders correctly on github.com with images embedded
4+ demo SKU briefs generate demo dataset (24+ images in S3)
Repo published at github.com/praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise with v1.0.0 tag
5-minute demo script rehearsable by interviewer
deliverables: README.md — setup, how-to-run, example I/O with image embeds, design decisions, assumptions/limitations, backlog
BACKLOG.md — future roadmap (PostgreSQL, Rekognition, multi-language, production scaling)
docs/design-decisions.md — deep-dive: model selection rationale, Pillow vs server-side, flat JSON vs DB, Claude Code as interface
inputs/demo_briefs/ — trail-mix-us.json, granola-bar-latam.json, energy-drink-apac.json, protein-bar-eu.json
outputs/demo/ — best images downloaded from S3 for README embeds
.claude/skills/generate-demo.md — runs all demo briefs with --model-tier final
docs/demo-script.md — 5-step flow: architecture → run-pipeline → view results → S3 → CI/CD
git tag v1.0.0 pushed to origin
success_criteria: Visiting github.com/praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise shows README with embedded images
All README sections present and accurate (verified by human review)
Demo script can be executed end-to-end in ≤5 minutes
CI/CD badge in README shows green
DECISION GATE G-005 PASSED: human confirms repo is demo-ready
team_allocation: Paul — final human review and approval (20%)
dependencies: P-004 complete — G-004 passed, CI/CD green on main
risks: README image embeds require outputs/demo/ images to be committed to repo — verify LFS not needed for PNG files of this size (~1-5MB)
Demo SKU briefs with --model-tier final invoke Nova Canvas (4 briefs × 2 products × 3 ratios × $0.04 = ~$1) — acceptable with unconstrained budget

phases.name: Enhancements (Optional)
objectives: Brand compliance checks via Claude Sonnet 4.6 LLM-as-judge
Regulatory content checks via synthesized JSON lookup table by region
Approval tracking appended to manifest.json (no separate database)
deliverables: src/pipeline/brand_checker.py — LLM-as-judge via AnthropicBedrock: returns {compliant, issues, confidence}
src/pipeline/regulatory_checker.py — JSON lookup: data/regulatory_requirements.json by region
tests/test_brand_checker.py, tests/test_regulatory_checker.py
manifest.json compliance section: {brand: {...}, regulatory: {...}}
success_criteria: Brand checker returns meaningful assessment for 2+ test images
Regulatory checker catches missing allergen disclosure for at least 1 configured test region
Manifest compliance section present after P-006 run
/pipeline-status shows compliance PASS/FAIL/WARN column
dependencies: P-005 complete — G-005 passed, repo published and demo-ready
risks: OPTIONAL PHASE: only start if P-005 is complete and interview is not imminent
Brand compliance LLM-as-judge results are non-deterministic — document as indicative, not authoritative

decision_gates.name: Bedrock Access Gate
criteria: aws bedrock list-foundation-models --profile pai-exercise includes amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0
aws bedrock list-foundation-models --profile pai-exercise includes anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6
aws sts get-caller-identity --profile pai-exercise returns account 730007904340

options.go: Proceed to P-001 coding (Claude Code scaffolding, CloudFormation)
options.conditional_go: Nova Canvas pending approval — use Titan V2 as temporary primary; switch to Nova Canvas when approved
options.no_go: AWS account not configured or Bedrock access request denied — must resolve before any pipeline code

decision_gates.evidence_required: Terminal output of aws bedrock list-foundation-models showing both model IDs

decision_gates.name: First Image Gate — Minimum Demo State
criteria: S3 output bucket contains ≥2 images (one per product, 1:1 ratio)
Human opens generated images and confirms text overlay is readable

options.go: Proceed to P-003 (all 3 aspect ratios, quality improvements)
options.conditional_go: Images generated but text overlay imperfect — ship as-is for minimum demo, iterate in P-003
options.no_go: Bedrock returning errors consistently or images are blank — diagnose before proceeding

decision_gates.evidence_required: Human has visually confirmed at least 1 generated image with readable product name

decision_gates.name: Full Requirements Gate
criteria: Full pipeline run produces ≥6 images (2 products × 3 aspect ratios) in S3
Human confirms all 3 aspect ratios are visually correct and text is legible
Manifest.json written to both S3 and local outputs/runs/

options.conditional_go: All 3 ratios functional but one layout imperfect — proceed if text is minimally readable
options.no_go: One or more aspect ratios producing blank/corrupt images — diagnose before proceeding

decision_gates.decider: Paul (human visual inspection of all 3 formats)
decision_gates.evidence_required: aws s3 ls s3://{output-bucket}/ --recursive showing front_label/, back_label/, wraparound/ directories

decision_gates.name: CI/CD Gate
criteria: GitHub Actions ci.yml shows green checkmark on main branch
GitHub Actions deploy.yml ran successfully on last main push

options.go: Proceed to P-005 (docs and demo preparation)
options.conditional_go: ci.yml passing but deploy.yml has credential issues — proceed to docs, fix deploy async
options.no_go: Unit tests failing or pip-audit blocking on high CVE — must fix before proceeding

decision_gates.evidence_required: Screenshot or URL of GitHub Actions tab showing green checkmarks on main

decision_gates.name: Demo Readiness Gate
criteria: Demo script can be executed in ≤5 minutes by Paul
git tag v1.0.0 pushed to origin

options.go: Submit repo link to interview panel — exercise complete
options.conditional_go: Repo functional but README images not embedded — submit with S3 links instead
options.no_go: Core pipeline not working (should not reach G-005 without G-002/G-003 passing)

decision_gates.evidence_required: github.com repo URL reviewed by Paul; demo-script.md rehearsed successfully

sprints.name: Foundation Setup
sprints.goal: Repo created, AWS stack deployed, Claude Code scaffolded, G-001 verified
tasks: gh repo create praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise --public --clone --gitignore Python
Create directory structure: src/, tests/, infra/cloudformation/, .claude/skills/, .github/workflows/
Write infra/cloudformation/stack.yaml (S3 × 2 with Block Public Access, IAM role, Budget alarm)
aws cloudformation deploy --stack-name pai-exercise --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM
python -m venv .venv && pip install boto3 pillow anthropic pytest ruff jsonschema pip-audit
Pin requirements.txt with exact versions
Write CLAUDE.md — project purpose, skills index, architecture summary
Scaffold 8 Claude Code skill stubs in .claude/skills/ (run-pipeline through generate-demo)
Write .claude/settings.json — PostToolUse lint, Stop test gate, PreToolUse Bash guard
Write .mcp.json — awslabs.aws-iac-mcp-server@latest (uv stdio), AWS Knowledge MCP (remote HTTP)
GATE G-001: verify Bedrock model access
git add . && git commit -m 'feat(phase-01): foundation complete' && git push

sprints.name: Pipeline Modules
sprints.goal: All 5 pipeline modules implemented and importable; SKU brief parses and validates

Write inputs/sample_sku_brief.json (2+ products, trail mix example)
Implement src/pipeline/sku_parser.py — parse_sku_brief + validate_sku_brief
Implement src/pipeline/asset_manager.py — check_s3_asset, download_s3_asset, upload_output, build_output_key
Implement src/pipeline/prompt_constructor.py — build_image_prompt + build_text_overlay_content
Implement src/pipeline/text_reasoning.py — AnthropicBedrock(aws_region='us-east-1') client wrapper
Implement src/pipeline/image_generator.py — generate_image with retry/backoff + 1×1 dry-run placeholder

sprints.name: Overlay, Output, and First Full Run
sprints.goal: Pipeline end-to-end: /run-pipeline produces ≥2 images in S3; G-002 human gate passed
tasks: Implement src/pipeline/text_overlay.py — apply_overlay for 1:1 layout (title, attributes, regulatory)
Implement src/pipeline/output_manager.py — write_manifest to S3 + local
Implement src/pipeline/run_pipeline.py — orchestrator for all products × 1:1 ratio
Update .claude/skills/run-pipeline.md to invoke run_pipeline.py
Run: /run-pipeline inputs/sample_sku_brief.json
GATE G-002: Paul opens images and confirms text overlay is readable
git commit -m 'feat(phase-02): core pipeline — SKU parser to S3 upload'

sprints.name: All 3 Ratios + Caching + Dry-Run
sprints.goal: 6+ images per run, S3 organized by format, caching working, G-003 human gate passed
tasks: Update image_generator.py: dimension map for 1:1 (1024×1024), 9:16 (576×1024), 16:9 (1024×576)
Update text_overlay.py: 9:16 layout (product top, description mid, regulatory bottom) + 16:9 layout (name left, attributes right, regulatory strip)
Update run_pipeline.py to iterate all 3 ratios × all products
Implement image caching: SHA-256 hash of (prompt+dimensions+model_id), ~/.cache/pai-pipeline/
Implement --dry-run mode end-to-end (env var PAI_DRY_RUN or flag)
Update .claude/skills/pipeline-status.md and view-results.md
Run full pipeline: verify 6+ images in S3 organized by format
GATE G-003: Paul inspects all 3 ratios
git commit -m 'feat(phase-03): all 3 aspect ratios, caching, dry-run, manifest'

sprints.name: CI/CD and Test Suite
sprints.goal: GitHub Actions green on main, full unit test suite passing, G-004 gate passed
tasks: Write tests/test_sku_parser.py, test_prompt_constructor.py, test_image_generator.py, test_text_overlay.py, test_asset_manager.py
Write tests/test_integration.py @pytest.mark.integration — full dry-run via subprocess
Write .github/workflows/ci.yml (SHA-pinned actions: checkout + setup-python)
Write .github/workflows/deploy.yml (AWS credentials via GitHub Secrets or OIDC)
Write Makefile with: make test, make lint, make audit targets
Run: ruff check src/ && pytest tests/ -v -m 'not integration'
git push origin main — trigger CI; verify GitHub Actions tab
GATE G-004: Paul confirms green checkmarks on main
git commit -m 'feat(phase-04): CI/CD, full test suite, auto-lint hooks'

sprints.name: Docs, Demo Data, Publish
sprints.goal: README renders with images on GitHub, demo rehearsed, v1.0.0 tagged
tasks: Write README.md (all required sections, image embeds from outputs/demo/)
Write BACKLOG.md (PostgreSQL, Rekognition, multi-language, production scaling)
Write docs/design-decisions.md
Create inputs/demo_briefs/ (4 SKU briefs)
Run: /generate-demo — 4 briefs × final tier
Download demo images from S3 to outputs/demo/
Write docs/demo-script.md
git tag v1.0.0 -m 'PoC complete — all exercise requirements met'
git push origin main && git push origin v1.0.0
GATE G-005: Paul reviews github.com repo and confirms demo-ready

milestones.name: Minimum Demo State — First Image in S3
Full Exercise Requirements Met — All 3 Ratios
PoC Complete — Demo Ready — v1.0.0 Tagged

critical_path: HUMAN GATE: Bedrock model access console enablement (amazon.nova-canvas-v1:0, titan-image-generator-v2:0, claude-sonnet-4-6) — MUST complete before BLOCK-1
HUMAN GATE: aws configure --profile pai-exercise — MUST complete before CloudFormation deploy
CloudFormation deploy → G-001 gate (Bedrock accessible) → BLOCK-2 pipeline modules
image_generator.py → text_overlay.py → run_pipeline.py → first full run → G-002 (MINIMUM DEMO STATE)
All 3 aspect ratios → caching → dry-run → G-003 → CI/CD setup → G-004
G-004 (CI/CD green) → README + demo data → G-005 → v1.0.0 tag → submission
NOTE: Claude Code scaffolding (CLAUDE.md, skills, hooks, MCP) is parallel to CloudFormation during BLOCK-1 — not on critical path
NOTE: Test suite (BLOCK-5) is critical for CI/CD gate G-004 but does not block image generation quality work in P-003

risk_register.risk: Bedrock model access not enabled before development starts — G-001 blocks all pipeline work
risk_register.mitigation: Enable Nova Canvas, Titan V2, and Claude Sonnet 4.6 in Bedrock console before first BLOCK-1 task. This is the highest-value pre-work step and takes ~5 minutes in the console.
risk_register.trigger: aws bedrock list-foundation-models does not show nova-canvas-v1:0

risk_register.risk: Nova Canvas text rendering quality insufficient for packaging label copy
risk_register.mitigation: Nova Canvas scored ImageReward 1.250 per Amazon Nova Technical Report (arxiv 2506.12103) — strong text-to-image fidelity for packaging use case. If quality is unsatisfactory: adjust prompt, use negative prompts, or accept Titan V2 for PoC demonstration.
risk_register.trigger: Generated images miss product name or attributes entirely in 3+ test runs

risk_register.risk: Pillow text overlay coordinate mapping for 9:16 and 16:9 ratios requires more iteration than estimated
risk_register.mitigation: Timebox text overlay per ratio to 20 minutes. If layout is imperfect after timebox, ship with functional (not pixel-perfect) overlay — PoC standard is demonstrability, not production quality. Move coordinate tuning to BACKLOG.
risk_register.trigger: Text overlaps critical image elements or is truncated on 2+ test runs per ratio

risk_register.risk: GitHub Actions CI/CD credential setup (OIDC or Secrets) requires additional human IAM configuration
risk_register.mitigation: Use GitHub Secrets (simpler) for initial deploy.yml setup. OIDC federation is preferred but adds 15 minutes of IAM Identity Provider setup in console. If OIDC is blocked, GitHub Secrets with short-lived key rotation is acceptable for PoC.
risk_register.owner: Paul (human IAM console action if OIDC chosen)
risk_register.trigger: deploy.yml GitHub Actions run fails with credential error on first push

risk_register.risk: GenAI API costs unexpectedly high due to demo data generation (final tier runs)
risk_register.mitigation: Budget is unconstrained. Expected total: ~$5. AWS Budget alarm at $25 provides early warning. All development runs use Titan V2 (dev tier, $0.12/run). Nova Canvas (final tier, $0.48/run) only for demo generation in P-005.
risk_register.trigger: AWS Budget alarm at $25 threshold fires

risk_register.risk: Claude Code hooks not triggering correctly — auto-lint or test gate not firing as expected
risk_register.mitigation: Verify hooks by editing a .py file and observing lint output in Claude Code after P-001. hooks in .claude/settings.json use PostToolUse on Edit|Write and Stop event — confirm Claude Code version supports these event types.
risk_register.trigger: Edit a Python file and lint hook does not run within 5 seconds

risk_register.risk: AnthropicBedrock text reasoning adds latency to pipeline, slowing iteration
risk_register.mitigation: Text reasoning (C-012) is optional enhancement to prompt construction — if it adds >5s latency per image, disable it and fall back to f-string templates for PoC scope. Claude Sonnet 4.6 typical P50 latency is <2s for short prompts.
risk_register.trigger: Per-image pipeline time exceeds 60s P50 (NFR-001) with text reasoning enabled

risk_register.risk: Scope expansion in P-005 (README details, demo data quality) delays G-005
risk_register.mitigation: Timebox P-005 to 1 hour. README follows a template from docs/design-decisions.md. Demo data uses /generate-demo skill — one invocation handles all 4 briefs. P-006 enhancements are strictly optional.

external.dependency: HUMAN ACTION: Enable Bedrock model access (Nova Canvas, Titan V2, Claude Sonnet 4.6) in AWS console — approximately 5 minutes
HUMAN ACTION: Run aws configure --profile pai-exercise — enter Access Key ID, Secret, region us-east-1
external.status: required before CloudFormation deploy in P-001

external.dependency: HUMAN ACTION: Verify gh auth status shows authenticated as praeducer; run gh auth login if not
external.status: required before GitHub repo creation in BLOCK-1

external.dependency: HUMAN ACTION: Visual inspection of generated images at G-002 (minimum demo state) and G-003 (all 3 ratios)
external.status: required gates — P-003 and P-004 cannot start without human approval

external.dependency: Target repo local path: C:\dev\pai-take-home-exercise (must exist before BLOCK-1)
external.status: created by gh repo create --clone in BLOCK-1 task 1

communication_plan.stakeholder_updates: AI-assisted self-directed PoC. Implementation agent (Claude Code) reports to Paul at each decision gate (G-001 through G-005). No external status updates required until G-005 (repo published for interview panel).
demo_schedule: Live demo to Adobe interview panel: 20-30 minutes covering architecture overview + live pipeline execution

Demo agenda: (1) Architecture overview 5min, (2) Code walkthrough via Claude Code skills 5min, (3) Live /run-pipeline execution 5min, (4) S3 output browsing 2min, (5) GitHub Actions CI/CD review 2min, (6) Q&A remaining time
Target repo: github.com/praeducer/pai-take-home-exercise (public, with README images and v1.0.0 tag)

decision_points.decision: Model selection confirmation: Nova Canvas (primary) vs Titan V2 (dev/fallback) — based on Bedrock access confirmation at G-001
decision_points.decider: Paul — verify nova-canvas-v1:0 accessible, not just Titan

decision_points.decision: GitHub Actions credentials approach: OIDC federation (preferred) vs GitHub Secrets (simpler) — based on available time and IAM console access at P-004
decision_points.decider: Paul — choose based on time available during P-004

decision_points.decision: Phase 6 (Enhancements) inclusion: brand compliance + regulatory checks — only if G-005 passes with time remaining
decision_points.decider: Paul — go/no-go based on interview timeline
